name: Cocktails & Spirits
description: Cocktail recipes, spirits, wine, and drinking culture

questions.q: A Bloody Mary is traditionally made with vodka and which juice?

source.notes: Also includes Worcestershire, Tabasco, celery salt. Debated origins: 1920s-1930s.

questions.q: A classic Piña Colada blends white rum, pineapple juice, and what creamy ingredient?

source.notes: National drink of Puerto Rico since 1978.

questions.q: A Manhattan cocktail is made with rye whiskey, a dash of bitters, and which fortified wine?

source.notes: Garnished with a cherry. Dry vermouth makes it a 'Dry Manhattan.'

questions.q: The Mint Julep, the official drink of the Kentucky Derby, is made with which spirit?

source.notes: Served in a silver or pewter cup with crushed ice and fresh mint.

questions.q: A classic Mojito gets its refreshing character primarily from mint and which spirit?

source.notes: Also includes lime, sugar, and soda water. Cuban origin.

questions.q: A Daiquiri in its simplest form combines rum, sugar, and the juice of which fruit?

source.notes: Named after a beach near Santiago de Cuba. Hemingway was a famous fan.

questions.q: A Tom Collins is a cocktail made with soda water, lemon juice, sugar, and which spirit?

source.notes: With vodka it becomes a 'Vodka Collins.' Named in 1870s bartender's guide.

questions.q: A White Russian cocktail combines vodka, cream, and which coffee-flavored liqueur?

source.notes: Popularized by 'The Big Lebowski' (1998). Without cream it's a Black Russian.

questions.q: Vodka is most closely associated as the national spirit of which country?

source.notes: Both Russia and Poland claim its invention. Name from Slavic 'voda' (water).

questions.q: A typical beer has an alcohol by volume (ABV) in what range?

source.notes: Light beers ~3-4%, craft IPAs often 6-8%, imperials 8-12%.

questions.q: Which bold red wine variety, originally from Bordeaux, is known for its strong tannins and full body?

source.notes: Cross of Cabernet Franc and Sauvignon Blanc. Most planted wine grape globally.

questions.q: In the United States, what is the legal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for driving?

source.notes: Utah is 0.05%. Commercial drivers: 0.04%.

questions.q: What is the legal minimum drinking age in the United States?

source.notes: National Minimum Drinking Age Act of 1984.

questions.q: Which U.S. Founding Father operated one of the largest whiskey distilleries in America at Mount Vernon?

source.notes: Produced ~11,000 gallons of whiskey in 1799, his most profitable venture.

questions.q: The expression 'Dutch courage' — meaning bravery from drinking — originated during which 17th-century war?

source.notes: English soldiers noted Dutch soldiers drinking jenever (gin) before battle.

questions.q: Bacardi, one of the world's best-selling rum brands, was originally founded in which country?

source.notes: Founded in Santiago de Cuba in 1862 by Don Facundo Bacardí Massó.

questions.q: A fortified wine has had extra alcohol added to it, usually in the form of what spirit?

source.notes: Examples: Port, Sherry, Madeira, Marsala. Typically 15-22% ABV.

questions.q: Acetaldehyde, a toxic byproduct of alcohol metabolism, is the primary chemical responsible for what unpleasant experience?

source.notes: Produced by the liver enzyme alcohol dehydrogenase.

questions.q: Vodka is typically distilled from grains or which other common source ingredient?

source.notes: Can also be made from grapes, beets, or corn. Smirnoff uses corn.

questions.q: In the U.S., what is the stricter BAC limit for commercial vehicle drivers?

source.notes: Half the standard 0.08% limit. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations.
